items.title: Mahindra & Mahindra – Q4 FY26 summary Strong earnings across Auto, Farm, Mahindra Finance and the growth businesses, with ROE at 20% and management c...
items.description_html:
Mahindra & Mahindra – Q4 FY26 summary

Strong earnings across Auto, Farm, Mahindra Finance and the growth businesses, with ROE at 20% and management calling FY26 a year of continued strong execution.

Business-wise breakdown

Auto: FY26 PAT grew 33% YoY to ₹7,842 crore. SUV volume grew 19% YoY to 1,118k, SUV revenue market share rose to 25.3% (+260 bps YoY), and LCV market share improved to 52.3%. Auto business margin improved to 10.9% in Q4 FY26. 100% PLI compliant

Farm: FY26 PAT grew 13% YoY to ₹4,298 crore. Domestic tractor volumes grew 24% YoY to 526k, market share improved to 43.6% (+30 bps YoY), and farm standalone PBIT margin rose to 19.8% in FY26.

Mahindra Finance: PAT grew 60% YoY to ₹2,855 crore; AUM grew 12% YoY to ₹1,34,000 crore, and GS3 improved to 3.41%. 99% secured portfolio. Udaan-important tech-platform for disbursals.

Tech Mahindra: PAT grew 14% YoY to ₹4,811 crore. TCV was $3,794 million and EBIT margin improved to 12.6% (+290 bps YoY), showing margin-led recovery.

Growth gems: Strong momentum in real estate, logistics, aerostructures, last mile mobility, hospitality and trucks & buses. 7x PAT move in Lifespaces and improving scale in logistics / EV / aero.

Key guidance & management commentary

Management is guiding for mid-single-digit industry growth in Auto & Farm, but expects Mahindra to do better because of product strength, market share gains and capacity additions. 15–18% SUV growth is possible if capacity ramps as planned.

Auto margins are expected to stay healthy, though the mix includes eSUV contract manufacturing which dilutes reported margin. Auto standalone margin was 10.4% for FY26. 10+ new launches in SUV and LCV by FY31

In Farm, the big focus is the new tractor range and product refreshes. (with transmission & power). Exited Sampo, Erkunt business units.

AI is now being treated as a real operating lever, with management citing ₹4,100 crore+ revenue potential in FY27 from AI-led adoption, alongside shorter time-to-market (10% reduced).

The strongest combination is market-share gains + margin expansion + high ROE. Auto is still the key earnings engine, Farm is stable with good margins, Finance is scaling with improving asset quality, and TechM is adding margin-led support.

Auto growth is backed by SUV leadership and EV traction, going to be less supply and more demand. No impact because of fuel prices fluctuations.
Farm is backed by new product cycles,
financial services improving with cleaner asset quality and scale.


Shashank Udupa - SEBI RA: Wonderful Results! - Il Update concall notes here by 4pm

Disc - Part of our FLUID C Smallcase

items.title: SJS delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 29.7% YoY and EBITDA margin expansion of 470 bps to 31%, significantly exceeding mana...
items.description_html:
SJS delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 29.7% YoY and EBITDA margin expansion of 470 bps to 31%, significantly exceeding management's ~27% guidance. The company outperformed the automotive industry by 2.7x and generated strong cash conversion (1.30x CFO/PAT).

Key risk is subsidiary drag where WPI continues to underperform. With robust execution, debt-free balance sheet, and active capacity expansion (CWIP +858% YoY), the company is well-positioned for continued outperformance.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 29.7% YoY in Q4 to ₹2,601.22 million

EBITDA margin expanded 470 bps YoY to 31% in Q4, exceeding ~27% guidance

PAT grew 44.9% YoY to ₹1,717.96 million

Operating cash flow to PAT ratio was 1.30x, indicating high-quality earnings

Outperformed automotive industry by 2.7x (41.9% vs ~15.7% industry)

Company remains virtually debt-free with strong balance sheet

Risk Factors

Subsidiaries contributed 40.9% of revenue but only 22.7% of PAT, creating margin drag

WPI (consumer durables) continues to underperform impacting group profitability

Other expenses grew 43.5% YoY, faster than revenue growth

Trade receivables grew 27.7% YoY, slightly faster than revenue

items.title: BREAKING: Global central bank gold purchases surged +36 tonnes in Q1 2026, to 244 tonnes, the highest since Q4 2024. This significantly exceeds the 5-...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Global central bank gold purchases surged +36 tonnes in Q1 2026, to 244 tonnes, the highest since Q4 2024.

This significantly exceeds the 5-year average of ~228 tonnes.

By comparison, the 5-year average in 2016-2020 was ~115 tonnes, or less than half the current level.

Central banks have now bought over +200 tonnes of gold in 10 out of the last 11 quarters.

Poland led Q1 purchases at +31 tonnes, bringing its total reserves to a record 582 tonnes, heading closer to its 700 tonnes target.

Uzbekistan followed at +25 tonnes and China at +7 tonnes.

World central banks are acquiring gold at an accelerated pace.

items.title: POONAWALLA delivered a exceptional Q4 FY26 with AUM growth of 71.1% far exceeding the 35-40% guidance, NIM recovering to 9.20% meeting the ~9% target,...
items.description_html:
POONAWALLA delivered a exceptional Q4 FY26 with AUM growth of 71.1% far exceeding the 35-40% guidance, NIM recovering to 9.20% meeting the ~9% target, and PAT turning positive at ₹541.81 Cr for FY26 vs loss in FY25. Operating leverage drove Cost-to-Income to 43.6% from 54.1% YoY while asset quality improved with GNPA at 1.44%.

The company raised ₹2,500 Cr via QIP post year-end, strengthening capital for continued growth.

Positive Factors

AUM grew 71.1% YoY to ₹55,951 Cr, beating guidance of 35-40% by a wide margin

NIM recovered to 9.20% in Q4, meeting management's ~9% guidance

PAT turned positive at ₹541.81 Cr for FY26 vs loss of ₹(98.34) Cr in FY25

Cost-to-Income ratio improved to 43.57% from 54.1% YoY showing operating leverage

GNPA improved to 1.44% from 1.84% YoY; NNPA to 0.74% from 0.85% YoY

Q4 PAT grew 308.8% YoY to ₹254.79 Cr

Provisions declined 24.3% YoY to ₹1,104 Cr from ₹1,458 Cr in FY25

Raised ₹2,500 Cr via QIP post year-end to strengthen balance sheet

Risk Factors

Finance costs rose 84.1% YoY, outpacing interest income growth of 77.2% YoY

Debt-Equity ratio increased to 4.67x from 3.2x in FY25 indicating higher leverage

CAR declined to 18.17% in Q3 from 22.94% in FY25 before QIP capital raise

Credit cost as % of AUM at ~2.5% still elevated compared to pre-pandemic levels

items.title: COFORGE delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 30% YoY and EBIT margin expansion of 431 bps to 16.6%, significantly beating the ...
items.description_html:
COFORGE delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue growth of 30% YoY and EBIT margin expansion of 431 bps to 16.6%, significantly beating the 15% exit rate guidance. The company exceeded its full-year EBIT margin guidance of 14% with 14.42% actual.

The ROW segment turnaround to profitability and strong free cash flow of 10,492 Mn further validate operational excellence. The completed Encora acquisition positions the combined entity to achieve $2 billion revenue target for FY27.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 30.05% YoY to 44,504 Mn
EBIT margin expanded 431 bps YoY to 16.6%, exceeding 15% guidance target

PAT grew 116.82% YoY to 6,662 Mn; EPS grew 133.72% YoY to 18.23

FY26 EBIT margin of 14.42% beat 14% guidance

ROW segment turned profitable: EBIT swung from -124 Mn to +220 Mn YoY

Free cash flow strong at 10,492 Mn, up 68.5% YoY

Encora acquisition completed for Rs. 221,935 Mn, targeting $2 billion FY27 revenue

Risk Factors

Trade receivables grew 54% YoY, outpacing revenue growth of 35.9%

Net loss on exchange fluctuations increased to 1,103 Mn in FY26 vs 281 Mn

Exceptional items material at 2,260 Mn (acquisition costs, labor code provision, legal costs)

Professional charges grew 65.5% YoY, faster than revenue

items.title: RT Chandra R. Srikanth: 🚨 Exclusive: Cognizant may cut 12,000-15,000 jobs globally; bulk of cuts in India IT services major Cognizant is weighing a...
items.description_html:
RT Chandra R. Srikanth
🚨 Exclusive: Cognizant may cut 12,000-15,000 jobs globally; bulk of cuts in India

IT services major Cognizant is weighing a reduction in workforce that could impact 12,000-15,000 employees worldwide, with the bulk of the layoffs expected in India, multiple sources told Moneycontrol.

On April 29, the Nasdaq-listed Cognizant said it expects to incur between $230 million and $320 million in severance costs under its newly announced Project Leap, without disclosing the number of potential job losses.

The company employs more than 357,000 people, with most, over 250,000, based in India.

The layoffs estimate is based on back of the envelope calculations using typical salary and severance assumptions across geographies.

In India, the average annual salary is assumed to be around Rs 15 lakh.

With severance ranging between four to six months, the cost per employee rises to roughly $50,000, meaning fewer employees can be impacted for the same level of provisioning.

Combining these assumptions across regions yields a global estimate in the range of 12,000 to 15,000 employees.

Sources said these calculations are indicative and based on broad assumptions around salary, tenure, and geographic mix, and the final number could vary depending on how the restructuring is executed.

With @shaw_reshab

https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/information-technology/cognizant-may-cut-12000-15000-jobs-globally-bulk-of-cuts-in-india-13908373.html

items.title: EBGNG delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with revenue growth of 34% (vs 20-25% guidance) and EBITDA margin expansion of 209 bps (vs 75 bps guidan...
items.description_html:
EBGNG delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with revenue growth of 34% (vs 20-25% guidance) and EBITDA margin expansion of 209 bps (vs 75 bps guidance). The company demonstrated superior execution with PAT growing 91% YoY while reducing net debt by 29%.

However, investors should monitor the negative operating cash flow of ₹2,153 Cr driven by aggressive working capital buildup, which was funded entirely by a ₹4,000 Cr equity raise.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 34.0% YoY to ₹18,910.75 Cr, beating guidance of 20-25%

EBITDA margin expanded 209 bps to 10.37%, beating guidance of 75 bps improvement

PAT grew 91.2% YoY to ₹1,320.16 Cr

Q4 EBITDA margin at 9.72% avoided repeat of Q4 FY25 margin compression

Subsidiaries contributed 69.75% of consolidated PAT showing strong international growth

Net debt reduced 29.3% to ₹2,875.08 Cr

Total equity expanded 234.3% YoY driven by retained earnings and IPO proceeds

Risk Factors

Operating cash flow deeply negative at (₹2,153.02) Cr, CFO/PAT ratio concerning at -1.63x

Inventory grew 52.7% YoY and receivables surged 205.9% YoY, far outpacing revenue growth

Other expenses grew 84% YoY in Q4, faster than revenue

Free cash flow negative at (₹2,381.52) Mn

Working capital investment funded entirely by ₹4,000 Mn equity infusion

items.title: AJANTPHARM delivered FY26 results in line with guidance on revenue (17.3% growth) and adjusted EBITDA margin (27.47%), with US Generics massively outp...
items.description_html:
AJANTPHARM delivered FY26 results in line with guidance on revenue (17.3% growth) and adjusted EBITDA margin (27.47%), with US Generics massively outperforming at 48.7% YoY vs high-teen guidance. However, Asia business significantly missed guidance with -1.3% de-growth, and cash conversion deteriorated sharply with CFO/PAT at 0.50x due to working capital stretch from US receivables.

The company remains net cash positive but faces near-term headwinds from FDA observations and weak cash flow generation.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 21.5% YoY in Q4 and 17.3% for FY26, meeting mid-teens guidance

US Generics grew exceptional 48.7% YoY, far exceeding high-teen growth guidance

Adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.47% was within guided 27% +/- 1% range

India business grew 13% YoY, outperforming IPM growth of 10%

Africa Branded Generics grew 14.8% YoY, meeting low double-digit guidance

Net cash position of ₹452.63 Cr remains strong despite reduction from FY25

PAT grew 14.7% YoY to ₹1,056 Cr for FY26

Risk Factors

Asia Branded business de-grew 1.3% YoY, significantly missing mid-single to high-single digit guidance

CFO to PAT ratio fell to 0.50x indicating poor cash conversion quality

Trade receivables surged 57% YoY stretching working capital significantly

Reported EBITDA margin compressed 193 bps YoY in Q4 due to forex losses

US ANDA filings at 5 vs target of 10-12, missing guidance significantly

FDA inspection at Paithan with 5 observations - potential regulatory risk

items.title: J&KBANK delivered strong Q4 FY26 results with PAT growth of 36.5% YoY, beating its own guidance on credit growth (17.7% vs 12% target), ROA (1.78% vs ...
items.description_html:
J&KBANK delivered strong Q4 FY26 results with PAT growth of 36.5% YoY, beating its own guidance on credit growth (17.7% vs 12% target), ROA (1.78% vs 1.20-1.25% target), and asset quality (GNPA 2.50% vs <3% target). Operating expense control drove margin improvement while capital adequacy strengthened.

Key risks include muted NII growth, declining other income, and funding pressure from deposit growth lagging credit growth.

Positive Factors

PAT surged 36.5% YoY to ₹797.80 Cr in Q4 FY26

Credit growth beat guidance - 17.7% achieved vs 12% target

ROA improved to 1.78% in FY26 from 1.32% in FY25

GNPA improved to 2.50% from 3.37% YoY, beating <3% target

Operating expenses declined 22.7% YoY to ₹836.08 Cr in Q4

Cost-to-income ratio improved to 56.18% from 57.64%

Capital adequacy ratio strengthened to 16.55% from 16.29% YoY

NPA provisions reduced 76.5% YoY to ₹6.92 Cr

Risk Factors

NII growth muted at 1.4% YoY, interest expense rose 7.8%

Other income declined 35.4% YoY in Q4 to ₹259.39 Cr

Retail Banking profit fell 18.6% YoY to ₹1,884.18 Cr

Operating cash flow turned negative at ₹-1,131.86 Cr in FY26

Deposit growth lagged credit growth by 640 bps, funding pressure

QIP process spilled over, not completed by March 2026

items.title: GHCL reported a challenging FY26 with PAT declining 24.3% YoY due to margin compression in the soda ash business, missing its 15% CAGR growth target. ...
items.description_html:
GHCL reported a challenging FY26 with PAT declining 24.3% YoY due to margin compression in the soda ash business, missing its 15% CAGR growth target. However, sequential improvement in Q4 (revenue +4.5% QoQ, EBITDA +9.4% QoQ) and strong cash generation (FCF ₹424 Cr, CFO/PAT 1.46x) indicate operational stabilization.

The Bromine & Vacuum Salt diversification projects missed the guided Q4 commissioning timeline, though capex continues with ₹449.56 Cr in CWIP. Balance sheet remains robust with net cash position.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 4.5% QoQ to ₹790.51 Cr showing sequential stabilization

Operating cash flow strong at ₹687.30 Cr with 1.46x CFO/PAT ratio indicating high-quality earnings

Free cash flow robust at ₹423.63 Cr after ₹263.67 Cr capex

Finance costs declined 54% YoY to ₹1.87 Cr due to deleveraging

EBITDA margin improved 80 bps sequentially from Q3 to Q4

Dividend payout policy enhanced from 15% to 25% of standalone PAT

Balance sheet remains strong with net cash position despite capex

Risk Factors

PAT declined 23% YoY in Q4 and 24.3% for full year FY26

EBITDA margin compressed 697 bps YoY to 23.9% due to negative operating leverage

Total expenses grew 8.2% YoY while revenue grew only 1.2% YoY

Bromine & Vacuum Salt projects missed guided end-Q4 FY26 commissioning timeline

Other income fell 41.7% YoY impacting overall profitability

FY26 revenue declined 3.75% YoY to ₹3,064.21 Cr due to challenging soda ash pricing environment

items.title: EMCURE delivered strong FY26 results with revenue growth of 16.6% beating guidance of 13-14%, driven by exceptional international business growth of 2...
items.description_html:
EMCURE delivered strong FY26 results with revenue growth of 16.6% beating guidance of 13-14%, driven by exceptional international business growth of 22.2%. Adjusted PAT grew 41.0% YoY with 1.0x cash conversion.

However, margin expansion of 80 bps missed the 150 bps guidance target, and debt increased 64.6% to fund the Zuventus acquisition. International business now represents 56.2% of revenue, positioning the company well for global expansion.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 16.6% YoY to ₹92,035.40 million, beating management guidance of 13-14%

International business grew 22.2% YoY to ₹51,770 million, exceeding mid-teens guidance

Adjusted PAT grew 41.0% YoY to ₹10,080 million with 36.1% YoY growth in Q4

Operating cash flow to PAT ratio at 1.0x indicating excellent cash conversion

Employee costs grew only 7.3% vs revenue growth of 16.6%, showing operating leverage

EBITDA margin expanded 80 bps YoY to 19.4% from 18.6%

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin expanded only 80 bps vs guidance of 150 bps, missing target by 70 bps

Total borrowings increased 64.6% to ₹12,040.83 million, missing debt-free target significantly

Domestic business grew only 5.2% YoY in Q4 vs 10.0% full year, soft Q4 performance

Working capital increased 35.3% YoY, with receivables up 28.0% and inventories up 24.1%

Other expenses grew 29.0% YoY in Q4, outpacing revenue growth significantly

Non-controlling interest reduced 86.4% due to Zuventus buyout, changing subsidiary structure

items.title: The loudest predictions of Indian IT's structural decline tend to come from people who have not spent any meaningful time inside an enterprise IT orga...
items.description_html:
The loudest predictions of Indian IT's structural decline tend to come from people who have not spent any meaningful time inside an enterprise IT organisation.
Not as a delivery lead.
Not as an account director.
Not as a transformation architect.
Not as the senior person who has had to land a cutover in production over a weekend with three regulators on the call.
Announcement comes, the commentary follows. While it is well-presented, mostly, technically correct. Bit, it is operationally weak.

Select section wants Industry to be impacted. It's mainly because of a position, perception they have about the industry and finally want that to be vindicated on.

The sector is undergoing through a reset, no doubt. If you think the industry has no strength to stand on, you will be proven wrong.

items.title: ALKYLAMINE Q4 FY26 showed mixed results with EBITDA margin expansion (43 bps YoY to 20.41%) driven by falling raw material costs, but overall FY26 rev...
items.description_html:
ALKYLAMINE Q4 FY26 showed mixed results with EBITDA margin expansion (43 bps YoY to 20.41%) driven by falling raw material costs, but overall FY26 revenue declined 2.3% due to weak demand from pharma and agrochemicals.

The company exceeded its own EBITDA margin guidance (20.59% vs 18-20% range) and maintains a strong balance sheet with zero debt. However, the force majeure event and inventory drawdown impacted profitability, and the forward outlook remains challenging with subdued end-market demand.

Positive Factors

FY26 EBITDA margin 20.59% exceeded management guidance range of 18-20%

Q4 EBITDA margin expanded 43 bps YoY to 20.41%

Cost of Materials Consumed declined 14.1% YoY in Q4

Operating cash flow strong at ₹238.55 Cr (CFO/PAT ratio 1.33x)

Debt-free balance sheet with Debt-to-Equity ratio of 0.00x

Current ratio improved to 3.12x from 2.61x

Risk Factors

Revenue declined 2.3% YoY for full year FY26

Force majeure impacted operations in Q4 (last 15 days)

Inventory drawdown of ₹23.37 Crores negatively impacted PBT

Effective tax rate increased to 26.07% from 25.15%

Working capital deteriorated with decrease in trade payables

items.title: Last 5 years have been extremely tough for the Chemical Sector De-stocking, weak demand, pricing pressure, global oversupply etc almost everything wen...
items.description_html:
Last 5 years have been extremely tough for the Chemical Sector

De-stocking, weak demand, pricing pressure, global oversupply etc almost everything went against the sector

Result - Many stocks are down 50-70% from their ATH

There was clear exuberance in 2021, that excess has now been rationalised

The easy China+1 narrative is already behind us.

But niche specialty chemical players with differentiated products, contract manufacturing, CRAMS, pharma intermediates, fluorochemicals, electronic chemicals, battery materials etc can see a better cycle.

So yes, the next 5 years can be much better than the last 5 years
But unlike 2021, this will not be a blind rally in every chemical stock

Do share which companies are worth studying at this point, which you think are sitting at an inflection point⏬

items.title: Marico delivered FY26 revenue growth of 25.7% meeting its guidance but missed operating profit growth target (8.8% vs double-digit) due to severe comm...
items.description_html:
Marico delivered FY26 revenue growth of 25.7% meeting its guidance but missed operating profit growth target (8.8% vs double-digit) due to severe commodity inflation causing 265 bps margin contraction. Sequential improvement visible in Q4 with gross margin recovering to 45% and EBITDA growth accelerating to 13.8% YoY.

International business was a star performer with 25% growth and resilient margins, offsetting domestic margin pressure. Strong cash generation (52.9% CFO growth) provides flexibility for integration of recent acquisitions. Key risk is subsidiary drag on consolidated profits and是否能实现 margin recovery in FY27.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 25.7% YoY to ₹13,611 cr, meeting management's over 25% guidance

International business delivered robust 25.1% YoY growth in Q4 with resilient 24.0% EBIT margin

Operating cash flow grew 52.9% YoY to ₹2,084 cr with 1.15x CFO/PAT conversion

Gross margin improved sequentially from 43.5% in Q3 to 45.0% in Q4 indicating easing cost pressures

Q4 EBITDA grew 13.8% YoY, accelerating from 11% in Q3, showing recovery momentum

Digital-first portfolio crossed ₹1,000 cr ARR target, driven by acquisitions

Free cash flow remained healthy at ₹1,313 cr after capex and acquisitions

Risk Factors

Full-year EBITDA margin contracted 265 bps to 17.10% due to severe input cost inflation

Cost of materials grew 35.5% YoY for FY26 and 52.8% YoY in Q4, significantly outpacing revenue

Operating profit growth of 8.8% missed double-digit guidance target

Subsidiaries were a 10.2% drag on PAT (₹179 cr lower than standalone)

India EBIT margin contracted 305 bps to 16.1% due to competitive intensity and cost inflation

Working capital tied up with inventories up 30.4% YoY

US chip export controls have delivered diminishing returns, restricting hardware but failing to contain China's AI capability. India, a consumer-integrator economy, risks being caught in the crossfire, dependent on a single supplier whose access terms shift with political winds.

In this Issue Brief, Pranay Kotasthane (@pranaykotas) examines the Nvidia-China debate from India's vantage point and charts a strategy around Pax Silica, GPU-agnostic middleware, and deliberate model pluralism.

items.title: Raymond's transformation into a focused engineering company post-demerger shows strong operational execution with 64.4% YoY segment EBIT growth and ma...
items.description_html:
Raymond's transformation into a focused engineering company post-demerger shows strong operational execution with 64.4% YoY segment EBIT growth and margin expansion in both core segments. Aerospace EBITDA margin entered the 22-25% target band, validating the high-barrier strategy.

However, significant cash flow deterioration (82% YoY decline in CFO, negative FCF of ₹7,207 lakhs) and debt buildup (47.5% YoY) raise concerns about capital efficiency. The disconnect between normalized PAT growth of 22.1% and reported 2.9% highlights restructuring headwinds that are non-recurring.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 13.6% YoY to ₹2,21,210 lakhs for FY26

Segment EBIT grew 64.4% YoY to ₹19,006 lakhs, significantly outpacing revenue

Precision Technology EBIT margin expanded 266 bps to 8.7% for FY26

Aerospace & Defence grew 26% YoY with EBIT margin at 12.4%

Aerospace EBITDA margin of 22.3% entered management's 22-25% target band

Normalized PAT grew 22.1% YoY to ₹6,351 lakhs after adjusting for exceptional items

Capex of ₹11,384 lakhs was in line with ₹10,000 lakhs annual guidance

Risk Factors

Operating cash flow collapsed 82.1% YoY to ₹4,177 lakhs

Working capital outflows of ₹22,715 lakhs (receivables + inventories)

Free cash flow negative at ₹(7,207) lakhs

Net debt position of ₹31,093 lakhs vs net cash of ₹9,386 lakhs in FY25

Total borrowings increased 47.5% YoY to ₹99,851 lakhs

EBITDA margin compressed 210 bps to 15.1% for FY26

Reported PAT grew only 2.9% YoY vs normalized 22.1% due to restructuring costs

items.title: Shanthi Gears reported a challenging Q4 with revenue declining 11.8% YoY and PAT down 27.6%, impacted by margin pressure from rising material costs. H...
items.description_html:
Shanthi Gears reported a challenging Q4 with revenue declining 11.8% YoY and PAT down 27.6%, impacted by margin pressure from rising material costs. However, the strong order book growth of 89% YoY and unexecuted order book of ₹349 Cr (67% of FY26 revenue) provides revenue visibility for FY27.

The company maintains a strong balance sheet with no debt and a current ratio of 3.71x. The Q4 sequential recovery (15.7% QoQ) suggests demand is recovering after H1 weakness.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue recovered 15.7% QoQ to ₹135.10 Cr after H1 weakness

Order booking grew 89% YoY in Q4 with unexecuted order book at ₹349 Cr (67% of FY26 revenue)

Company remains debt-free with net worth up 9.2% YoY to ₹440.06 Cr

Current ratio strong at 3.71x with cash and bank balances of ₹75.21 Cr

Free cash flow positive at ₹17.12 Cr despite capex doubling to ₹52.98 Cr

Dividend maintained at ₹5/share for FY26
Risk Factors

Revenue declined 11.8% YoY in Q4 and 14.2% for full FY26

PAT declined 27.6% YoY to ₹16.27 Cr in Q4

EBITDA margin contracted 320 bps YoY to 19.6% due to material cost pressure

Material cost as % of sales jumped from 44.2% to 53.2% in Q4

Free cash flow significantly down from ₹66.52 Cr to ₹17.12 Cr due to higher capex

Exceptional charge of ₹3.22 Cr in Q4 related to new Labour Codes

items.title: PNB delivered mixed FY26 results with credit growth beating guidance (13.72% vs 11-12%) but NII significantly missing (+7% guidance vs -1.92% actual)....
items.description_html:
PNB delivered mixed FY26 results with credit growth beating guidance (13.72% vs 11-12%) but NII significantly missing (+7% guidance vs -1.92% actual). Asset quality showed marked improvement with GNPA at 2.95% and NNPA at 0.29%, both multi-year lows. Cost-to-income improved to 51.82% and capital adequacy strengthened to 17.74%.

The bank prioritized balance sheet strength over margin expansion, which paid off in asset quality but resulted in ROA missing the >1% target at 0.89%.

Positive Factors

Credit growth of 13.72% exceeded management guidance of 11-12%

Operating profit grew 9.2% YoY in Q4 meeting 8-9% guidance

Cost-to-income ratio improved to 51.82% from 54.60%

GNPA improved to 2.95% from 3.95% YoY, NNPA to 0.29% from 0.40%

Capital adequacy ratio improved to 17.74% from 17.01% YoY

Net profit grew 12.07% YoY to ₹5,591.64 Cr

Operating cash flow was 1.50x PAT indicating strong cash conversion

Risk Factors

NII declined 1.92% YoY significantly missing +7% growth guidance

ROA of 0.89% missed >1% target

NIM pressure continued with Interest Expended growing faster than Interest Earned

Treasury operations profit declined 26% YoY

NY Times (today) “Forecasters say a powerful El Niño weather pattern could form later this year, with a chance of becoming one of the strongest in three decades. The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration says there’s a roughly 60% chance of an El Niño developing between May and July.”

CNA Singapore (today) “Last week, the United Nations' weather & climate agency said El Nino conditions could develop as soon as May to July.

The World Meteorological Organization - meanwhile, said early signs indicated the event could be particularly strong, with some dubbing the impending event a "super El Nino"

If true, not great news for most Asian countries !

items.title: VOLTAMP delivered 11.35% revenue growth for FY26 meeting its guidance targets, but suffered severe profitability collapse with Q4 PAT down 50.5% YoY a...
items.description_html:
VOLTAMP delivered 11.35% revenue growth for FY26 meeting its guidance targets, but suffered severe profitability collapse with Q4 PAT down 50.5% YoY and normalized EBITDA margin contracting 1024 bps to 9.27%. Raw material cost inflation (materials as % of sales jumped from 60.2% to 84.6% in Q4) drove margin compression of 359 bps for the full year, contradicting management's stated focus on 'profitable growth'.

Operating cash flow collapsed 36% due to ₹15,223 Cr working capital absorption (inventories +64% YoY), resulting in weak cash conversion of 0.46x. The company remains on track with its greenfield EHV capacity expansion (CWIP +3063% YoY) and maintains a debt-free balance sheet, but execution quality was mixed with profitability targets clearly missed.

Positive Factors

Full-year revenue grew 11.35% YoY to ₹2,153.69 Cr, meeting guidance targets

Company remains debt-free with strong equity of ₹1,791.80 Cr (89% of assets)

Greenfield EHV facility in Jarod on track with CWIP up 3063% YoY

New land acquisition of ₹25 Cr near Vadodara approved for future expansion

Dividend of ₹100 per share (₹101.17 Cr total) declared despite Q4 weakness

Risk Factors

Q4 PAT declined 50.53% YoY to ₹47.90 Cr, full-year PAT down 6.15%

Normalized EBITDA margin collapsed 1024 bps in Q4 to 9.27%

Cost of materials as % of sales spiked from 60.2% to 84.6% in Q4

Working capital outflow of ₹15,223 Cr led to 36% YoY drop in operating cash flow

Cash conversion ratio deteriorated to 0.46x from 0.67x in FY25

Management's 'profitable growth' objective missed - margin compression 359 bps for full year

items.title: M&M delivered strong Q4 FY26 results with revenue up 28.9% YoY and PAT up 41.7% YoY, beating its own guidance on SUV growth (29.7% vs mid-high teens t...
items.description_html:
M&M delivered strong Q4 FY26 results with revenue up 28.9% YoY and PAT up 41.7% YoY, beating its own guidance on SUV growth (29.7% vs mid-high teens target), tractor growth (20.3% vs 10-12% target), and Farm ROCE (29.4% vs 18%++ target).

Financial Services was a standout with PBIT doubling and margins expanding 809 bps. Concerns include margin compression from inventory swing and EV portfolio drag on auto margins.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 28.9% YoY to ₹54,891.55 crore in Q4 FY26

PAT attributable to owners grew 41.7% YoY to ₹4,667.57 crore in Q4

Auto segment revenue grew 32.4% YoY, beating guidance of mid-high teens

Farm Equipment revenue grew 20.3% YoY, beating guidance of 10-12%

Financial Services PBIT doubled (+111.2% YoY) with margins expanding 809 bps to 22.3%

Auto PBIT margin expanded 132 bps to 9.3%

Operating cash flow improved to ₹11,657 crore from ₹3,176 crore in FY25

Debt-Equity ratio (ex-FinServ) improved to 0.04x from 0.07x

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin contracted 90 bps YoY to 18.71% due to ₹855 crore inventory drawdown vs ₹1,259 crore build-up in Q4 FY25

Farm Equipment PBIT margins contracted 181 bps to 10.1% in Q4

Auto blended PBIT margin at 8.9% vs 10% target (excl. EVs), indicating EV portfolio drag

Farm Equipment revenue declined 12.9% QoQ in Q4, showing normalization after strong year

items.title: RT Normal Guy: India reserves sit at $698+ billion but that number hides a lot. Gold is $131 billion of that, you cannot sell gold fast to pay for cru...
items.description_html:
RT Normal Guy
India reserves sit at $698+ billion but that number hides a lot.

Gold is $131 billion of that, you cannot sell gold fast to pay for crude imports. RBI forward book is now $104 billion in net short dollar positions, a record.

Strip both out and usable foreign currency drops to around $449 billion. That is roughly where India was in 2021.

The Iran war did not create a reserves problem. It exposed that the real liquid buffer was much thinner than the top-line number

FCNR redux sounds reasonable but the maths is completely different from 2013.

Back then US rates were near zero, so RBI could offer concessional swaps cheaply and still pull in $26 billion.

Today the Fed funds rate is 3.5-3.75%, making every hedged dollar far more expensive for RBI to absorb.
FCNR-B flows have already collapsed 87% this year to under $1 billion.

Even if RBI reopens the window, the cost per dollar raised will eat into its own balance sheet.

Import cover as a metric assumes exports go to zero. The net BoP deficit of $30 billion against $698 billion in reserves gives you a 5% drawdown rate per year, which is manageable in isolation.

The problem is three things hitting at once - crude above $110, FPI outflows crossing $20 billion in 2026, and the rupee already down 5.5% year-to-date.

Each one drains dollars through a different channel simultaneously.


Ira Dugal: India’s central bank is studying ways to boost dollar flows, including the now-famed FCNR route.

While headline level of FX reserves looks adequate, adjusted for gold and forward book, they provide about 7-odd months of import cover.

The thinking is on. Reuters @kalrajs23 and

items.description_html:
RT Flexible Capital ⭐
Using a PMS for MFs is like hiring Einstein to teach grade 8 physics

The tax angle isn't the point: the investor directly and via PMS wrapper pays taxes whenever funds are being switched. It's actually the double payment for advisory fee: on the PMS and on the MF (via TER)

A PMS makes sense for direct stocks esp if the manager can outperform constantly (net of fees). Otherwise an RIA or even a good MFD can do the job if you want to stick to MFs

items.title: Deepinder Goyal says AI is our friend, not a threat - "Some people wonder whether AI chat interfaces will become the new front door for commerce, shif...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal says AI is our friend, not a threat -

"Some people wonder whether AI chat interfaces will become the new front door for commerce, shifting demand away from apps like ours.

We've seen such a story play out before. Google spent over a decade trying to pull transactional behaviour into its own platform. Google Flights. Google Hotels. Google Shopping. Restaurant ordering is built into Google Maps. They had the largest demand surface on earth, billions of searches a day, and the strongest distribution advantage in the history of the world.

And yet Booking dot com is still here. Expedia is still here. Amazon is still here. The vertical apps that consumers had built habits around didn't get displaced.

The reason is simple. General-purpose interfaces are good for general-purpose queries. They are poor interfaces for complex, high-frequency, habitual transactions. Ordering food involves browsing menus, comparing restaurants, ratings, and delivery times, substituting items, and tracking orders in real time.

And more fundamentally, consumer behaviour is the hardest thing in the world to change. People who order dinner on Zomato four times a week, or groceries on Blinkit every other day, are not going to reroute those habits through a chat window. The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI assistants are a real but early segment, and currently, nowhere close to disrupting our business.

Brand associations are even harder to change than habits. When someone is hungry, they think Zomato. When they need groceries within minutes, they think Blinkit. When they want to go out, they think District. This is by design. It is why we run separate apps and focus on creating super brands, rather than a single super app. Each brand owns a specific trigger in the customer's mind. That mental real estate, built over years of consistent delivery on a specific promise, is not something that gets dislodged easily.

Also, most of our business happens in the offline realm. AI will not replace the need to move food through traffic. It will not stock shelves in a dark store. It will not manage crowd flow at a 50,000-person event. It will not build cold chain infrastructure or negotiate with suppliers in tier 2 cities. The physical world doesn't get disrupted by language models.

Nevertheless, if something similar didn't work in the past, it doesn't mean it won't work in the future. Agentic commerce is similar to, and also different from, Google's attempt at owning transactions. At this point, we believe there is nothing to panic about. But we will keep our eyes open, without an iota of complacency or wishful thinking, and edit the contours of our business where needed to make it even more defensible in the long term.

Now, what AI actually does for us –

We are one of the largest deployers of AI in India. Demand prediction, route optimisation, supply-chain management, customer experience, fraud detection, catalogue quality, and partner support already use AI 4 across our platforms. These systems are trained on billions of real interactions, where the cost of getting something wrong — a late delivery, a wrong order, a failed refund, an unavailable item- is immediate and measurable.

But the more important question is not how AI helps us reduce costs. That is useful, but not enough. The sharper question we ask ourselves is: how does AI expand the market?

In our businesses, markets expand when more people can participate — more customers, more delivery partners, more restaurants, more sellers, more brands, and more local businesses. AI reduces the friction required for each of them to participate.

For customers, conversational interfaces let customers express intent in full sentences rather than two-word search queries, which is a far richer signal to convert against. It lowers the friction caused by static search and filters into intent-led discovery, especially for older users, non-native language speakers, first-time online buyers, and tier 2 and 3 city consumers who may struggle with app flows. Zomato’s Healthy Mode and Natural Language Search (NLS) are early examples of this. They could not have been built without AI.

For delivery partners, AI will make onboarding, training, and day-to-day guidance conversational and personalised, thereby expanding the earning opportunity to people who may otherwise struggle with app flows, language, documentation, or lack of confidence.

For restaurant partners and sellers, AI will make high-quality business guidance available from day one — the kind of growth-guidance that historically our teams could provide to a limited set of partners.

AI lets us serve people and businesses that were previously too hard, too small, too fragmented, or too operationally expensive to serve well. Operational efficiency will follow, but it is not the ambition. Our moat remains the physical world. AI makes that moat wider by allowing more of India to participate in it

Src - Deepinder Goyal Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter, no reco

items.title: The world is using more US Dollars than ever: Offshore US Dollar deposits held in banks outside the US are up to a record ~$14.5 trillion. This is +22...
items.description_html:
The world is using more US Dollars than ever:

Offshore US Dollar deposits held in banks outside the US are up to a record ~$14.5 trillion.

This is +220% more than the ~$4.5 trillion held at the beginning of the century.

By comparison, only ~$3.5 trillion worth of euros are held in offshore banks, outside the Eurozone.

And, the Fed and domestic commercial banks hold over $19.0 trillion.

This means offshore US Dollar deposits are now equivalent to~43% of US domestic bank deposits, with no other currency coming close to this percentage.

Global demand for USD is incredibly high.

items.title: RT Dhirendra Kumar: Re The pitch reads well. The math underneath does not. The PMS fee sits on top of the mutual fund expense ratio, not in place of i...
items.description_html:
RT Dhirendra Kumar
Re The pitch reads well. The math underneath does not.
The PMS fee sits on top of the mutual fund expense ratio, not in place of it. All-in: 1.1 to 2%. A direct MF portfolio does the same job at 0.5 to 1%.
Tax pass-through is a feature of mutual funds, not the PMS wrapper. The moment the PMS rebalances between schemes, the demat books a redemption and tax follows.
Profit sharing without a hurdle rate is not alignment. The manager keeps 20% of the upside and bears none of the downside. Investors pay carry for beating zero.
A HNI with 50 lakh can buy 4 to 6 direct funds, pay an RIA a flat fee, and keep most of what the wrapper would have taken. The structure pays the provider better. Not the investor.

items.title: BREAKING: The ratio of US leading to coincident economic indicators is now down to 0.84, matching the 2008 Financial Crisis low. This comes as the Lea...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The ratio of US leading to coincident economic indicators is now down to 0.84, matching the 2008 Financial Crisis low.

This comes as the Leading Economic Index (LEI) fell -0.6% MoM in March, posting its 7th monthly decline out of the last 8.

The Conference Board Leading Economic Index (LEI) tracks forward-looking data, including consumer expectations, manufacturing orders, weekly hours, and initial jobless claims.

At the same time, the Coincident Economic Index (CEI) measures current economic conditions in real time, such as nonfarm payrolls or personal income, excluding government social security or unemployment payments.

This ratio is now on track for its 5th consecutive annual decline, the longest streak on record.

In the past, such depressed levels have never occurred outside of a recession.

The US economy and the stock market are moving in opposite directions.

items.title: Software companies are flooding the US private credit industry: Software loans account for ~20% of all loans deployed by Business Development Companie...
items.description_html:
Software companies are flooding the US private credit industry:

Software loans account for ~20% of all loans deployed by Business Development Companies (BDCs).

BDCs are publicly traded firms that lend to small, mid-sized, and distressed US businesses, offering retail investors access to private credit markets.

Software exposure has now DOUBLED since 2016.

By comparison, Commercial Services accounts for ~19%, with Commercial Products and Healthcare Services representing just ~9% and ~6%, respectively.

Furthermore, private credit funds classify some software loans under different sector labels, meaning true exposure could be significantly higher.

This makes it difficult for investors to accurately assess the amount of risk concentrated in a single sector.

Private credit exposure is becoming highly concentrated in software.

items.title: The US auto loan crisis is accelerating: The average amount owed by underwater car borrowers rose to ~$7,200 in Q1 2026, the highest on record and the...
items.description_html:
The US auto loan crisis is accelerating:

The average amount owed by underwater car borrowers rose to ~$7,200 in Q1 2026, the highest on record and the 4th consecutive annual increase.

Over the last 4 years, the average amount owed by negative equity car borrowers has risen +71%.

Overall, ~30% of car buyers who traded in a vehicle in Q1 had negative equity.

This comes as pandemic-era vehicles, bought at peak prices, have lost value faster than borrowers can pay down the loans.

This compounds existing pressure on auto buyers amid elevated vehicle prices and interest rates.

Auto credit stress is spreading.

items.description_html:
Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins.

EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), even as:
– Aluminium & metals prices rose ~41%
– Battery and electronics costs remained volatile

What helped:
• Better sourcing & inventory planning
• Use of alternate materials (light rare-earth magnets)
• Shift to LFP batteries
• Product design changes

A good example of how execution can offset cost pressures.

items.title: Shocking stat of the day: Adjusted Stablecoin volume is projected to reach $719 TRILLION by 2035, according to a new report from Chainalysis. This pro...
items.description_html:
Shocking stat of the day:

Adjusted Stablecoin volume is projected to reach $719 TRILLION by 2035, according to a new report from Chainalysis.

This projection is assuming organic growth alone. When factoring in macro catalysts, Chainalysis says this figure could approach $1.5 quadrillion.

In fact, Stablecoin payment volumes are on pace to match Visa and Mastercard’s off-chain transaction volumes somewhere between 2031 and 2039.

This comes amid a massive shift toward Stablecoin infrastructure, with Western Union, $USDPT, Fidelity, $FIDD, Meta, and many other Fortune 500 companies preparing launches.

The ecosystem is also evolving, such as Jupiter's $JUPUSD, which returns yield back to the ecosystem and has been attracting large inflows.

Stablecoin adoption is skyrocketing.

items.title: US inflation expectations are surging: The US 10-year breakeven rate is up to 2.47%, the highest since February 2025. Excluding Q1 2025, this is the h...
items.description_html:
US inflation expectations are surging:

The US 10-year breakeven rate is up to 2.47%, the highest since February 2025.

Excluding Q1 2025, this is the highest level since October 2023.

In turn, 1-year inflation expectations are up to 3.26%, the highest since September 2022.

Furthermore, 2-year inflation expectations are up to 2.81%, the highest since November 2022.

All while US consumer 12-month inflation expectations remained above 6% for the 2nd consecutive month in April.

Inflation is back.

items.title: RT Gary Marcus: The case against OpenAI is getting markedly stronger now that Musk is off the stand. Why? Musk’s lawyer is interrogating OpenAI found...
items.description_html:
RT Gary Marcus
The case against OpenAI is getting markedly stronger now that Musk is off the stand.

Why?

Musk’s lawyer is interrogating OpenAI founder Greg Brockman, making clear that OpenAI sold its mission as a nonprofit for public benefit, and not just to Elon but to many big figures in Silicon Valley, seeking *donations* to establish the company as a nonprofit.

This case is *not about Elon*. It’s about the bait and switch that OpenAI pulled, raising money as a nonprofit and then switching after ChatGPT hit bit.

Yes, the company misled Elon, but also misled many many others, from the state of California to other early “donors”, to early employees and the public.

The question is whether that is ok.

items.title: Markets in Taiwan and South Korea are making history: The total market cap of Taiwan's stock market is up to a record $4.5 trillion, surpassing Canada...
items.description_html:
Markets in Taiwan and South Korea are making history:

The total market cap of Taiwan's stock market is up to a record $4.5 trillion, surpassing Canada’s $4.4 trillion for the first time.

Taiwan’s stock market value has also exceeded that of the UK for the first time 2 weeks ago.

This comes as Taiwan’s market cap has surged by +$2.7 trillion or +150% over the last 12 months.

At this pace, Taiwan could overtake India and become the 5th largest world market as soon as next month.

Meanwhile, South Korea's stock market cap is up to a record $4.1 trillion, having overtaken the UK last week, and on track to surpass Canada this month.

Collectively, the two markets have added +$4.6 trillion in market value over the last 12 months, driven by AI-related stocks.

AI is transforming Taiwan and South Korea into a global economic power.

items.title: From the Kotak Institutional Equities - Strategy Note by Sanjeev Prasad, MD & Co-Head. "With no major state election scheduled until early 2027, the g...
items.description_html:
From the Kotak Institutional Equities - Strategy Note by Sanjeev Prasad, MD & Co-Head.

"With no major state election scheduled until early 2027, the government enters a relatively election-free corridor of 10 months.

We expect the government to firmly focus on managing India's weakening macro environment, amid
(1) elevated crude oil prices,
(2) risks to food inflation from a sub-par monsoon and
(3) a widening current account deficit.

In our view, policymakers may focus on
(1) rationalizing energy subsidies, (2) accelerating trade diversification,
(3) finalizing the India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement and
(4) accelerating certain slow-moving reforms."

items.title: RT CSEINDIA: 📢 New video out! India generates over 1.7 lakh tonnes of daily waste, but nearly a quarter of it simply disappears into our surroundin...
items.description_html:
RT CSEINDIA
📢 New video out!

India generates over 1.7 lakh tonnes of daily waste, but nearly a quarter of it simply disappears into our surroundings.

But the crisis is not inevitable. Nationwide organic waste management systems can drastically reduce the garbage landfills by treating organic waste scientifically.

In this film, CSE's Solid Waste Management programme and @down2earthindia’s Multimedia team travel to Indore, Pune, Panjim, Thiruvananthapuram, and Bhubaneswar. We explore how Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across India are tackling both fresh and legacy waste through science-backed, community-driven, and sustainable organic waste management systems.

Discover their unique IEC (Information, Education & Communication) models, decentralised composting systems, citizen engagement strategies, and how they are turning the garbage crisis into a climate opportunity.

Watch now: https://youtu.be/y8BJxVEVoLA

items.description_html:
Milky Mist Dairy Food, a largely bootstrapped 30-year-old company, has been valued post-money at over Rs 9,300 crore or close to $1 billion.

It has raised Rs 482 crore in a pre-IPO round from Singapore’s Temasek, benchmarking its valuation ahead of the IPO.

The transaction includes a primary infusion of Rs 357 crore and a secondary sale of Rs 125 crore by founder-promoters Sathish Kumar T and Anitha S.

The promoters will continue to hold a 90% stake after this fundraising.

items.title: RT KV Iyyer - BHARAT 🇮🇳🇮🇱: Late Shri Manohar Parrikar Ji once narrated his ordeal. "I'm from Parra, a village in Goa, so we're called 'Par...
items.description_html:
RT KV Iyyer - BHARAT 🇮🇳🇮🇱
Late Shri Manohar Parrikar Ji once narrated his ordeal.

"I'm from Parra, a village in Goa, so we're called 'Parrikars.' My village is famous for its watermelons. When I was a child, the farmers there held a 'Watermelon Eating Contest' in May, after the harvest. All the children were invited and asked to eat as many watermelons as they wanted.

Many years later, I went to IIT Mumbai to study engineering. Then I returned to my village after 6.5 years. I went to the market to look for watermelons. But they were gone. The ones I found were very small.

I went to meet the farmer who used to hold the 'Watermelon Eating Contest.' Now his son had taken his place. He still held the contest, but there was a difference. When the old farmer offered us watermelons to eat, he would ask us to spit the seeds into a bowl. We were forbidden to chew the seeds. He was collecting seeds for the next crop.

We were, in effect, unpaid child laborers.

He would keep his best watermelons for the competition, using them as the best. He obtained good seeds, which produced even bigger watermelons the next year. When his son arrived, he thought the larger ones would fetch a higher price in the market, so he started selling the larger ones and keeping the smaller ones for competition. The next year, the watermelons grew smaller, and the next even smaller. A watermelon generation lasts one year.

In seven years, Parra's best watermelons were wiped out. In humans, a generation changes every 25 years. In 200 years, we will realize the mistakes we were making in educating our children.

Selecting good seeds, that is, talent, is a huge task in itself. Due to irrelevant ideas and useless things, our good watermelons will go to market, leaving us with useless, inferior seeds.

We must think about this in today's context.

items.title: W&C stocks showing good momentum today🟩 RR Kabel reported a solid set & is catching up in terms of valuations, as it was trading at a significant d...
items.description_html:
W&C stocks showing good momentum today🟩

RR Kabel reported a solid set & is catching up in terms of valuations, as it was trading at a significant discount compared to peers (Polycab & KEI).

Delivered a strong FY26, with the wires & cables segment continuing to drive overall growth.

Management highlighted that demand remained healthy across domestic & export markets, supported by infrastructure, housing, industrial activity & B2B expansion.

Company remains on track with its Project Rise roadmap, which targets 18% CAGR in W&C & 25% CAGR in FMEG, translating into 2.5x cumulative EBITDA growth over 3 years (FY25-28)

W&C contributed 90% of total REV in FY26, and the segment delivered strong growth, supported by higher volumes, better pricing, export momentum & operating leverage.

For FY27, management expects W&C volume growth to remain in the 16-18% range, with cables expected to grow faster than wires.

Cable contribution will increase in the coming quarters, and this mix improvement should support better margins over time.

On margins, Management said the company has already achieved around 130 bps improvement in FY26 under Project Rise.

The target is to improve W&C margins by 300 bps by FY28. For FY27, management has guided for around 9.5% margin, and by FY28, the target is to reach towards double digits.

Margin improvement will be driven by scale benefits, higher cable mix, better product mix, operating efficiencies & export growth.

The 1,200 Cr capex plan for FY26-28 is progressing as planned. Capacity addition will happen gradually every 6 months rather than one large capacity addition.

A key upgrade is that RR Kabel currently manufactures up to 66 KV cables, and after the new capex, it will be able to manufacture up to 220 KV cables.
Data centers are also emerging as an important growth opportunity.

FMEG remained weak in FY26, but losses have reduced.
FY27 FMEG growth is expected to be supported by fans, switches, switchgears, appliances & other categories, and management expects FMEG to achieve breakeven in FY27.

It is already under our coverage from lower levels, not a reco to buy/sell.

items.title: In FY26, 11 Cr Indians completed transactions worth over $10 billion through Blinkit, District, and Zomato The company Deepinder Goyal accidentally st...
items.description_html:
In FY26, 11 Cr Indians completed transactions worth over $10 billion through Blinkit, District, and Zomato

The company Deepinder Goyal accidentally started, now touches how India eats, shops, and goes out. It took 18 years to get to $10 billion (per year).

This doubling to $20 billion annual NOV will take less than two years from here, as per his guidance!

Similarly, it took 16 years to reach Adjusted EBITDA profitability in FY24. From there, Eternal expects to reach $1 billion of Adjusted EBITDA, hopefully by FY29

The Interesting Part? Zomato started as a restaurant listing website. They spent nine years iterating: reviews, discovery, table reservations, and international expansion. They didn't even start delivering food until 2017. The business that processes billions today didn't exist for the first nine years!

Src - Deepinder Goyal Q4FY25 Letter to Eternal Shareholders, no reco

items.title: RT Ishaq Dar: Pleased to announce that twenty-two crew members from the seized Iranian container vessel, “MV Touska”, have been safely evacuated to ...
items.description_html:
RT Ishaq Dar
Pleased to announce that twenty-two crew members from the seized Iranian container vessel, “MV Touska”, have been safely evacuated to Pakistan.

The individuals were safely flown in Pakistan last night and will be transferred to Iran today. The Iranian ship is also being backloaded to Pakistani territorial waters for its return to its original owners after necessary repairs.

These actions are being coordinated with the support of both the Iranian and the US authorities and surely constitute an important confidence-building measure.

Pakistan expresses its appreciation to the United States and Iran, and remains committed to facilitating dialogue, diplomacy, and mediation for the regional peace and security.

items.title: Royal Enfield 350cc: 1-month waiting period. TVS iQube EV: can only meet 50% of demand. Both raised prices in April. Zero demand destruction. My take:...
items.description_html:
Royal Enfield 350cc: 1-month waiting period.

TVS iQube EV: can only meet 50% of demand.

Both raised prices in April. Zero demand destruction.

My take: India just crossed the income threshold where people stop buying what they need and start buying what they want.

China hit $2,500 per capita GDP in 2007.

Durables grew 20%+ annually for the next decade after that.

India crossed $2,500 per capita in FY25-26.

The dealer data is telling you what the quarterly results haven't yet.

Waiting lists and supply constraints in premium products = the thesis is working.

Not a stock call. Just an observation on where we are in the cycle.

items.description_html:
President Trump’s offer to 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz looks less like a neutral evacuation plan than a test of Iranian red lines !?

Trump has framed it as a humanitarian move to free hundreds of vessels but the operational logic military ?

Once U.S. warships begin escorting commercial traffic through waters - which Iran has sought to control - any Iranian resistance becomes a reason to escalate !

PS: Remember, Iran has already been selectively allowing or negotiating the movement of some ships - so U.S.'s sudden need to do this, risks a direct US/Iran confrontation.

Maybe this is the intention. Lets see.

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Bernstein on Kotak BK Market Perform, TP Rs 500 Kotak closed FY26 on a strong note, with a beat across most operating metrics . NIM ex...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Bernstein on Kotak BK
Market Perform, TP Rs 500
Kotak closed FY26 on a strong note, with a beat across most operating metrics .
NIM expanded ~13 bps QoQ — the highest amongst the top 4 PVBs — while credit costs normalized to 39 bps (-25 bps YoY) on the back of broad-based asset quality improvement.
Loan growth held up at ~16% YoY, and stable opex growth helped offset weaker NOI to drive RoA back above 2%.
RoE at 12.3%, however, remained below the other large PVBs, weighed down by elevated capital buffers.

UBS on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 500
Q4 PAT beat driven by higher PPOP and lower credit cost
Loan/Deposit growth at 3.2%/5.5% QoQ; CASA ratio up 200bp QoQ
Management expect one time ECL transition impact at <2% of net worth
At current valuations (1.5x March 2027E P/BV vs 1.5x - 1.9x for private peers), risk reward looks favourable given expected acceleration in PPOP growth, improvement in asset quality, range bound NIMs & momentum in non-lending business

JPM on Kotak BK
OW, TP Rs 476
delivered a steady 4Q with PAT at Rs 40.3bn (+13% y/y, RoE: 12.1%) coming largely in-line with JPMe.
Core performance was strong with broad-based beats across NII (+1% vs. JPMe), Opex (-3% vs. JPMe) and loan loss provisions (-35% vs JPMe) partially offset by muted non-interest income (-2% y/y, on unwinding of NDF positions and muted card growth).
NII growth of 8.1% y/y (+4.1% q/q) was among the best in the large private bank peer group, with core NIMs (4.54%) holding steady q/q on deposit re-pricing.
Reported NIMs rose by 13bps q/q to 4.67%, on day count benefit in 4Q.

Nomura on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
4Q26: Strong NIM delivery, cautious guidance
Sharp asset quality improvement
Raise FY27-28F EPS by ~2%, led by lower credit costs and opex
Expect RoA/RoE to improve to 2%/12% by FY28F (vs. 1.9%/11% in FY26), with strong EPS CAGR of 18% over FY27-28F

Jefferies on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 450
Q4 standalone profit of Rs40bn (13% YoY) was ahead of est with lower credit costs.
Loans grew by 16% and NII by 8%; growth in CASA was healthy at 16%.
Credit quality is improving with better trends in MFI & credit cards.
Still trim earnings by 3-4% as factor in lower NIM guidance due to higher cost of deposits
Vals are reasonable at 14x FY27E standalone adj. PE.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: The wealth gap has never been wider: US private sector financial assets relative to US GDP are up to a record 6.7x. This means that the total value of...
items.description_html:
The wealth gap has never been wider:

US private sector financial assets relative to US GDP are up to a record 6.7x.

This means that the total value of stocks, bonds, and other financial instruments held by the private sector has never been larger relative to the real economy.

This also surpasses the previous all-time high of 6.3x seen in 2021 after one of the sharpest market recoveries in history.

The size of private sector financial assets relative to GDP has more than DOUBLED since the 1970s low.

When financial assets outpace the real economy, the wealthy get richer, and workers get left behind.

Own assets or be left behind.

items.title: RT Camus: Brain scans are revealing early dementia-like changes in kids and teens from heavy screen use. 60 Minutes Australia reported toddlers spendi...
items.description_html:
RT Camus
Brain scans are revealing early dementia-like changes in kids and teens from heavy screen use.

60 Minutes Australia reported toddlers spending just 2–3 hours daily on devices already show abnormal white matter development. Teens averaging 6–8 hours display widened brain ridges and thinning in key areas — patterns that mirror early Alzheimer’s.

Excessive screens appear to weaken neural pathways that normally strengthen through real-world movement, play, and face-to-face interaction.

We’re also seeing the first IQ drops in recorded history, plus a nearly 400% rise in early-onset dementia signs among 35–44 year olds. Correlation, not proven causation — but devices are the major new variable.

This is one of those reports that makes you rethink default habits. The convenience of screens is undeniable, but the potential long-term brain impacts on developing kids are hard to ignore.

We may be unintentionally running a massive experiment on the next generation’s cognitive health.

Are we underestimating the risks of heavy screen time, or is this concern overblown?

items.title: What did I read this week 1. On April 20, the President of USA signed the Presidential Determination 2026-10, a Defense Production Act (DPA) Section 3...
items.description_html:
What did I read this week

1. On April 20, the President of USA signed the Presidential Determination 2026-10, a Defense Production Act (DPA) Section 303 determination for grid infrastructure, equipment, and supply-chain capacity.

Thereby, officially classifying conductors, transmission lines, transformers, substations, high voltage lines & capacitors & CRGO core used in transformers as items which are essential to national defence. The current waiting for distribution transformers in USA has gone to 13 months vs the historical average of 3 months & for power transformers the lead time is 42 months vs the historical average of 15 months.

This trend is visible financially in order book of GE Vernova, Eaton, Bloom energy & the GRID ETF. For EATON the order book increased by 16% YOY in Q4 results. Primarily driven by Data centre and demand for other electrical goods. For GE Vernova the Quarterly order book increased by 71% YOY, & back logs for GAS turbines has gone to 100 GW.

2. India's power demand hit a record high of 256.1 GW on April 25, 2026, driven by intense early summer heatwaves and economic growth. This peak demand surpassed the 250 GW record from May 2024, with expectations to reach 270 GW+ soon. The grid successfully met this surge using increased coal-fired generation and solar energy. Increased cooling needs (air conditioners) during April-June are the primary drivers for surging power usage.

3. Something which I do on every weekend is to check the list of industries which are strongly outperforming the benchmarks and displaying relative strength from here:- https://www.stockscans.in/market-scans

The two industries apart from power sector that caught my attention were the Metals and mining space & the capital markets. For case study I will cover one business from each sector briefly-

A) Post Vedanta's demerger. The Aluminium business & Power business will be worth tracking. The Aluminium business will have higher captive consumption of Bauxite going forward from FY27 which can increase their ebitda margins. In the power business this year they have commissioned around 1.6 gigawatt of capacity at Athena, Meenakshi. So, total capacity as of now up and running is 4.2 gigawatt. This is expected to go to around 4.8 gigawatt by end of H1 of the next year. Long term plan in place is to put additional 10 to 12 gigawatts of power capacity.

B) Nippon India Asset management: Nippon is a market leader in ETFs with 21.4%+ market share and many ETFs like Gold bees, Nifty Bees, Silverbees etc have the highest trading volumes. ETFs funnily is a business with network effects. More volume=more investors=More liquidity=lower impact cost=better reflection of underlying assets value=more investors=lesser impact cost=more liquidity. Passives as a category is growing at 30%+ since last 5 years in India. Worth studying.

4. Eli Lilly posted a big beat-and-raise in Q1’26, with 56% revenue growth and EPS up >150% driven mainly by explosive GLP‑1 demand in diabetes/obesity. Growth was volume-led despite pricing pressure, and management raised full-year revenue and EPS guidance, underscoring confidence in multi‑year GLP‑1 upside. An oral GLP‑1 (Foundayo) approval plus strong retatrutide and other late‑stage data deepen the pipeline, but investors should still watch U.S. pricing/regulatory risk and Lilly’s ability to keep scaling manufacturing to meet unprecedented demand.

Dr. Reddy’s just secured Health Canada’s first-ever approval for a generic Ozempic (semaglutide) injection, opening a key G7 beachhead for its global GLP‑1 strategy with a May launch window and aggressive pricing expected. Behind the scenes, OneSource Specialty Pharma is the CDMO workhorse scaling and manufacturing the formulation from its US‑FDA approved Bengaluru facility, while Shaily Engineering supplies the high‑volume GLP‑1 injector pens that will carry not just Dr. Reddy’s semaglutide, but potentially multiple global GLP‑1 generics over the next few years.

5. AUTO continues to fire:

Auto Volumes Apr’26:

▪️ PVs:Domestic Industry volumes grew ~20% YoY led by Maruti (+32%) and Tata Motors (+31%). Maruti witnessed broad-based strength across UVs, compact and mini segments. Hyundai (+17%) and M&M (+8%) reported relatively moderate growth, while Toyota continued to outperform (+21%).

▪️ CVs: Domestic CV volumes (ex-AL) remained healthy (+16% YoY) supported by infra-led freight movement. Tata Motors outperformed significantly (+28%) driven by strong SCV/pickup demand (~40% growth). VECV and M&M LCV grew 9% and 7%, respectively.

▪️ 2Ws: Domestic 2W volumes (ex-Bajaj) grew ~30% YoY led by Hero MotoCorp (+85%) and Royal Enfield (+37%). TVS grew 8% with strong EV traction (+36%), though motorcycle volumes remained impacted by supply constraints. Exports grew 19% YoY but remained mixed across OEMs.

6. CDMOs like Navin & Acutaas had a strong finish to the financial year 26. Both of their CDMO business growth is led by Darolutamide & launches of new CDMO molecules.

For Navin, the CDMO business crossed 541 crore of revenues and they are guiding for $100 Million in FY27.

For Acutaas they maintain their guidance of 1000 crore CDMO revenue for FY28, and encouragingly the Semiconductor chemical business reported solid margins and growth after multiple Quarters of destocking. They are doing a capex of nearly 200 crores for setting up the Semiconductor chemicals plant in South Korea.

7. The capital markets index is a rising part of the NSE 500 index and its share in the NSE 500 has grown from a mere 0.1% 10 years ago to 0.84% 5 years ago, and now stands at over 2.5%. The number of companies in the capital market segment increased from just 3 companies 10 years ago, to 10 companies 5 years ago, and now stands at 18 companies as per MOSL's concall.

There are tonnes of more learnings and I will keep sharing them as the results season has just started. Do let me know in the comments section if you loved these learnings and will like for us to continue this series :)

items.title: Atlassian's earnings calls is a great read. While Microsoft moved from per seat to usage based pricing, which is very logical, but JIRA for e.g., make...
items.description_html:
Atlassian's earnings calls is a great read. While Microsoft moved from per seat to usage based pricing, which is very logical, but JIRA for e.g., makes zero sense to move away from seat based pricing. In this case, at least, the seat compression is not visible.

Atlassian isn't reducing tokens by being clever with prompts. They're reducing tokens because with Jira/Confluence they & their clients have spent 20 years capturing structured relationships between work items, people, code, and knowledge. When Rovo (or any agent) needs context, it does a graph lookup, instead of a vector dump. This reduces dependance on token hungry RAG that enterprises use.

SAP has its own Knowledge Graph. In an enterprise, that's the moat AI cannot replace.

items.title: Oil was $70 in February. It's $108 today. That's +54% in 9 weeks. Most people think this is a "crude oil problem." It's not. It's an everywhere proble...
items.description_html:
Oil was $70 in February. It's $108 today.

That's +54% in 9 weeks.
Most people think this is a "crude oil problem." It's not. It's an everywhere problem.
Here's how it actually hits you 👇

- ATF (aviation fuel) is crude-linked.
- IndiGo just cut international capacity by 17% for May.
- When airlines shrink capacity, ticket prices go up.
- When ticket prices go up, discretionary travel drops.
- When travel drops, hotels, tourism, retail all feel it.

And this is just ONE transmission channel. Steel, cement, freight, fertiliser — all move with crude.

The market is treating this like a commodity story. It's a margin story for almost every sector in India.

This is what warflation looks like. Not stagflation. Not a recession. A supply shock that hasn't finished transmitting yet.

Grade A office demand has exceeded new supply every year since 2022

In 2025, demand stood at 72 Msf vs new supply of 57 Msf

For 2026, demand is expected at 70-75 Msf vs supply of 60-65 Msf, implying continued supply gap

Flex adoption rising structurally
- Flex stock expected to cross 100 Msf by 2027
- Flex penetration expected to improve from 10% in 2025 to 14% by 2027

Demand drivers
- 55-65% occupiers expected to use flex by 2027
- GCCs driving 40-50% of Grade A demand
- 70-80% of flex demand is enterprise led
- Tenures are now increasing to 3-5 years vs 1-2 years earlier

items.title: Germany's reluctance to invest in productive infrastructure isn't a sign of thriftiness, nor does it help Germany keep its debt burden low. If the inv...
items.description_html:
Germany's reluctance to invest in productive infrastructure isn't a sign of thriftiness, nor does it help Germany keep its debt burden low. If the investment is productive – i.e. if it creates more economic value than it costs in economic resources – then almost by definition the investment will cause GDP to rise by more than the debt rises, and German's associated debt burden will actually decline.

The point is that if German infrastructure is in bad enough shape to have become a constraint on productivity growth – and almost everyone agrees that it is – then borrowing to fund infrastructure investment will not only accelerate productivity growth, it will also reduce the debt burden by creating more than enough additional real income to service the additional debt.

Granted that it is difficult for a large economy to change institutions and growth models, including fear of debt, that have been in place for decades, but sclerosis isn't strategy, and as East Asian economies fight to continue increasing their share of global manufacturing, while the US works to reverse the decline in its own manufacturing share, it is precisely countries like Germany, for whom manufacturing currently represents a much larger share of the economy than in the rest of Europe, that will pay the highest cost for doing nothing.
https://www.wsj.com/world/europe/germany-has-the-cash-to-revitalize-its-economy-but-cant-seem-to-spend-it-5bd38dfe?st=db3BS1

RTAs & depositories have not really participated meaningfully in this recovery

On the other hand, brokers, exchanges, asset & wealth managers have seen a steep rally from the lows

RTAs & depositories were already trading at premium valuations, even after correcting 30-40% from their highs multiples are still not very reasonable

At the peak, valuations had become absurd, with some trading at 80x (NSDL & Kfin)

One caveat to keep in mind:
In a downcycle, depositories will always look optically expensive because part of their earnings is linked to market activity. When transaction volumes, IPO activity & corporate actions slow down, near term earnings get impacted, making valuation multiples look elevated

items.description_html:

items.title: RT Parimal: Before the world knew the power of Big Pharma, a journalist in a tiny lab in Bombay created a substance so potent it triggered a trade war...
items.description_html:
RT Parimal
Before the world knew the power of Big Pharma, a journalist in a tiny lab in Bombay created a substance so potent it triggered a trade war with London. It was a yellow grease that did not just soothe headaches but funded a movement, bypassed British blockades, & became 1 of the few Indian products to make the Empire's own medicine look like scented water.

Unlike other brands started by chemists, Amrutanjan was founded by Kasinadhuni/Kasinathuni Nageswara Rao, a man who was primarily a journalist & a freedom fighter. In the late 1800s, the pain balm market in India was a British monopoly. If your head throbbed, you bought imported ointments. Rao saw this as a tax on pain. He retreated into a lab & perfected a formula that was significantly more potent than anything coming out of London.

The British tried to push their own balms like Vicks/early menthol rubs as sophisticated & odorless. They attempted to smear Amrutanjan as primitive because of its overpowering scent. Rao leaned into the scent. He realized that in a country where literacy was low, a brand could not just be a name, it had to be an experience.

He distributed free samples at music concerts (Sabhas) & religious festivals. By the time the British tried to patent the market for pain relief, the entire Indian public had already associated the smell of camphor & menthol with trust. The British balms felt alien & weak compared to the sensory explosion of the yellow tin.

The smell of Amrutanjan... that piercing, camphor-heavy aroma became the literal scent of the freedom struggle. If you walked into a room & it smelled of Amrutanjan, it was a silent signal: A patriot is present. It was a scent the British police could not arrest, yet it was everywhere.

The British had a Patent Medicine Tax that made imported drugs expensive. However, by classifying Amrutanjan as an Ayurvedic Proprietary Medicine, Rao managed to navigate a complex legal gray area. He essentially used the British legal system against itself. By proving his ingredients were ancient yet his manufacturing was modern, he avoided the crippling taxes that applied to purely Western drugs, while maintaining a price point (initially 10 annas) that made British imports look like daylight robbery Rao fought back not just in the market, but in the press. He used the profits from the balm to fund Andhra Patrika, 1 of the most influential anti-British newspapers.

The British were literally paying for their own downfall. Every time a British officer’s wife bought a jar of Amrutanjan for a migraine (because it worked better than the London balms), she was inadvertently funding the printing of revolutionary literature that called for the end of the Raj.

By the 1930s, this Indian yellow grease was being exported to Indian diaspora & locals in South Africa & Ceylon. It became a global symbol of Eastern Wisdom defeating Western Chemistry. It was 1 of those few occasions, an Indian OTC (Over the Counter) product achieved cult status internationally w/o a single pound of British investment.

In fact, the yellow tin became so iconic that it did not need a label in the villages. The color & the smell were the brand. It was a biological Swadeshi. While others were fighting with words, Rao was fighting with molecular relief.

items.title: While his approach isn't entirely wrong & we may not necessarily be able to lead in this race, owning enough of the stack for resilient autonomy is an...
items.description_html:
While his approach isn't entirely wrong & we may not necessarily be able to lead in this race, owning enough of the stack for resilient autonomy is an absolute, uncompromising necessity.

> Own critical pieces of digital infra such as identity management
> Sovereign fine tuned models
> Domestic compute build up

In the age of geopolitical instability, not having foundational autonomy is very risky. Having indigenous alternatives to critical parts of digital stack is a necessity. Controlling, protecting those parts is even higher necessity.

During WW2, U.S. didn't drop a nuke to destroy, they did to make a statement. Back then only 1 country had it. What's the parallel today? It's AI & what can be controlled with it. During galwan valley clash, Chinese hackers did manage to plant a malware, India's power grid infra was targetted. RLDC, SLDCs were targetted. NSE faced 40cr+ cyber attacks in a single day during Sindoor.

Bottom line, India needs to build autonomy as much as possible. It's not to win the race but to build strong resilient future.


Nandan Nilekani: India doesn't need to lead the world in building the most advanced AI models. But it must lead in ensuring benefits of AI are widely shared.

@rvenk and I have an op-ed in The @EconomicTimes

https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/opinion/et-commentary/its-popular-mechanics-101-indias-edge-lies-in-using-ai-to-enhance-human-productivity-not-replace-it/articleshow/130586820.cms
